✦   Our Story   ✦

"We do not charge authors for reads, edits, or illustrations. We offer a royalty split and a small advance."

McGuffin Press was born from a simple conviction: great stories deserve a champion. We are a small, forward-thinking traditional press that bets on authors and illustrators who have something genuine to say.

We publish children's books and adult fiction with equal passion — believing the best storytelling transcends age. Whether a picture book a child carries into adulthood, or a novel that returns a grown reader to wonder.

No reading fees. No hidden costs. Just honest editorial partnership and a royalty split that respects the work.

McGuffin
Press
Auctores · Illustratores · Lectores
About the Founder

Being an author has been a rewarding experience. I have been a student, a mentor, a critique partner, and a published author — done book fairs, signings, and author school visits. Through it all, I have learned a great deal.

While I bid the sun farewell as it sets on the "actively writing" chapter of my life, I write a new chapter — helping the sun rise for fellow authors and illustrators. I want to carve a path for their success.

Onto the next! Allow me to introduce McGuffin Press.

What We Believe

The McGuffin Promise

Three principles that govern every manuscript we read, every author we sign, every book we send into the world.

No Fees, Ever

We never charge for manuscript reads, editorial notes, or illustrations. We only win when you do.

Honest Partnership

A royalty split and a modest advance. We're a small press — we say that clearly. What we lack in scale we make up in dedication.

Stories That Matter

Children's books and adult fiction chosen because they have something genuine to say. Great storytelling has no age limit.

Limited & Collectible

Special Editions

Collector's editions, signed copies, and limited runs — for readers who want something truly extraordinary.

Our Roster

Authors

The voices behind our books — writers who trusted us with their stories.

Lorraine Hawley
Middle-Grade Fantasy Author
Children's · Middle Grade

Born in California and now adventuring through Arizona, Lorraine Hawley is a retired paramedic and licensed nurse. A fantasy author, she delights in stories where courage, friendship, and self-discovery collide. Author of Curious World of Dandy-Lion, Lorraine hopes to ignite wonder and share magic with readers everywhere.

Visit Website →

Coming Late 2026
Medical Picture Book
Children's

Our next author will be announced soon. Stay tuned for an exciting addition to the McGuffin Press family.

Visual Storytellers

Illustrators

The artists who bring our stories to life — because in a picture book, illustration is half the soul.

Jocie Salveson
Designer, Painter & Book Illustrator
Children's Books · Picture Books

Jocie Salveson believes the world is her blank canvas. Born and raised in North Dakota, she's lived in Germany and Japan, and now resides in the Pacific Northwest. She brings her imagination and creativity wherever she goes — one can make art anywhere, and that's what she intends to do!

Visit Website →
Mark Sean Wilson
Illustrator of Map Magic & More
Children's Books · Cover Art · Technical Illustration

Mark Sean Wilson attended the Walt Disney School of Animation and Platt College for Graphic Design, graduating in 2002 with honors. In his 25 years of creating he has generated a vast portfolio spanning logos, storyboards, film editing, technical illustration, and children's books.

Visit Website →

Open to Voices

Submit Your Manuscript

"We look forward to hearing from you — and to reading every word you send."

McGuffin Press accepts children's books (picture books through middle grade) and adult fiction. No reading fees. No hidden costs.

We offer a royalty split and a small advance commensurate with our small press budget. Transparency from the first query.

✦

Electronic submissions only. Include a query letter, synopsis, and the first three chapters or complete picture book text.

What We're Looking For

Manuscript Wishlist

"These are the stories we lie awake dreaming about."

📖

Middle Grade Fantasy

Stories where young heroes discover courage they didn't know they had.

🌙

Children's Picture Books

Whimsical, warm, and wonder-filled. Stories that help children feel seen.

✦

Adult Fiction

Literary fiction with heart. Stories about ordinary people in extraordinary moments.

⚕️

Medical Picture Books

Books that help children understand health and hospitals with honesty and warmth.

🗺️

Adventure & Discovery

Stories driven by curiosity, exploration, and the thrill of finding something new.

❧

Underrepresented Voices

Stories from perspectives that don't get enough shelf space.

❖   The Story Behind the Press   ❖

About the Founder

Being an author has been a rewarding experience. During that time, I ran the gamut to build what Malcolm Gladwell and his book, Outliers, suggest: the ten-thousand-hour rule of deliberate practice needed to achieve “mastery” in any complex skill. However, I do not accept that I have mastered anything. Yet.

I have been a student, a mentor, a critique partner, and a published author. I have enjoyed growing, learning, and having a seat at the table with gracious book enthusiasts and fellow authors. I have been represented and, on submission, done book fairs, signings, and author school visits. Through it all, I have learned a great deal.

It is never easy to accept our limitations. While I may be comfortable writing into my nineties (never say never), for now, I seek time away from my computer and writing to help others achieve their dreams and spend precious time with my family and grandchildren.

So...

While I bid the sun farewell with grace as it sets on the “actively writing” chapter of my life, I write a new chapter, helping the sun rise for fellow authors and illustrators. I want to carve a path for their success.

Onto the next!

Allow me to introduce McGuffin Press. A small (for now) traditional press. We do not charge authors for reads, edits, or illustrations. We offer a royalty split and a small advance (to match our small press budget).

I am not confused by the difficulty of my new path. Small presses are not easy. They are not cheap. It was the difficulty that I, along with many fellow authors and illustrators, endured that led me to recognize a need.

I am not alone in trying to carve out a place for authors. I am, and will always be, thankful to the lovely small press that chose me and my middle-grade book.

The decision to start McGuffin Press with my own book (Release date: April 14, 2026) was to ensure that any mistakes would be at my peril. I knew I owed it to future McGuffin authors to have a tried experience. Going through the process of releasing this manuscript also helped me move forward, ripping off that shelved bandaid that many authors understand and riding into that sunset, waving my hat and hollering, Yippee!

For future reference, I will mark any books published by a founder under McGuffin with the founder’s mark. All others will be traditionally published.
